title: Head of Product (B2B SaaS)
description_excerpt: This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Head of Product (B2B SaaS) in India.This role is a high-impact leadership position focused on shaping and scaling a product suite powering influencer-led growth and performance marketing infrastructure. You will own end-to-end product strategy, execution, and delivery across multiple product lines, with a strong initial focus on building and scaling a core flagship product. The environment is fast-paced, highly iterative, and deeply customer-driven, requiring strong product instincts and the ability to operate in ambiguity. You will work closely with engineering, design, and leadership teams to translate customer insights into scalable product solutions. The role blends strategic thinking with hands-on execution, from roadmap ownership to sprint planning and product delivery. You will also play a key role in defining product metrics, improving user experience, and ensuring consistent shipping velocity. This is a leadership opportunity to build category-defining SaaS infrastructure in a rapidly evolving market.AccountabilitiesOwn the end-to-end product vision, roadmap, and execution across multiple B2B SaaS product lines, ensuring alignment with company strategy and customer needs.Lead customer discovery efforts, including interviews, user research, beta testing, and ongoing feedback loops to deeply understand user pain points and churn drivers.Drive product prioritisation, trade-offs, and sprint planning in close collaboration with engineering and design teams.Own the development and scaling of a core product (initial focus area), ensuring strong execution from concept to delivery.Define product requirements, write detailed specs, and ensure high-quality execution across design, engineering, and UX.Build, track, and optimise key product metrics such as activation, retention, engagement, and usage.Monitor market trends, competitive landscape, and ecosystem shifts (DTC, Shopify, affiliate marketing, influencer growth) to inform product strategy.Ensure high standards of user experience, design consistency, and product usability across all releases.Lead and mentor product and design teams, fostering a culture of ownership, speed, and quality.Collaborate closely with leadership and cross-functional teams to align product direction with business objectives.Requirements8+ years of product management experience, ideally in B2B SaaS environments.Proven experience building products from 0→1, with strong execution and scaling capabilities.Strong product judgment with the ability to make clear prioritisation and trade-off decisions.Deep customer-first mindset with strong user research and discovery skills.Strong design sensibility with attention to UX, usability, and product quality.Experience working in fast-paced, high-ambiguity environments requiring ownership and autonomy.Familiarity with DTC, Shopify, influencer marketing, or affiliate ecosystems is highl

title: Principal Product Manager
description_excerpt: About the Company:At Playlist, life's richest moments happen when people step away from screens to move, connect, explore, and play. We're building the definitive platform for intentional living, connecting people with inspiring experiences in fitness, wellness, and beyond. With popular brands like Mindbody and ClassPass, Playlist empowers businesses and individuals, making it effortless for aspirations to become actions. Join us in reshaping technology's role to foster meaningful, real-world connections.The Role You’ll Play:At the intersection of technology and global wellness, you’ll lead our international payments expansion. This role is about more than integrations and regulations—it’s about creating seamless, compliant experiences that empower businesses to grow and thrive across borders. Define and lead our global payments expansion strategy, aligning PSP integrations, market priorities, and compliance milestones Launch local payment methods and regional PSPs across key markets, owning the process from evaluation to go-live Build expertise in complex regulatory frameworks (like PSD2, Verifactu, NF525, and e-Invoicing), ensuring we stay ahead of local requirements Partner with Engineering to shape the global payments stack, from multi-PSP orchestration to routing logic and risk controls Collaborate with Compliance, Finance, and GTM teams to streamline reconciliation, settlement, and cross-border payouts Set success metrics for payment acceptance, reliability, and cost efficiency across markets Represent Payments in global initiatives, championing international enablement across product and platform teams Stay plugged into global payments trends, using competitive insights to inform product direction and decision-making____________________________________________________________________________________Experience You Bring: 8–10+ years of product management experience, with deep focus in global or cross-border payments Proven track record of integrating PSPs and enabling local payment methods across multiple regions Strong technical fluency—you speak API, understand payment flows, and can collaborate closely with Engineering Deep knowledge of authorization, settlement, and reconciliation processes, plus a solid grasp of merchant/PSP dynamics Familiarity with compliance frameworks like PCI-DSS, PSD2, and regional invoicing mandates Strategic mindset with a sharp eye for data—you're comfortable defining metrics and making decisions based on insight Exceptional communication and collaboration skills across Product, Engineering, Compliance, and Finance teams Bonus: Experience with B2B SaaS or platform-based paymentsHave we piqued your curiosity?Sound like the role for you? We’d love to hear from you! Even if you’re not 100% sure about potential fit, we still encourage you to apply. We’re looking for the right person, not the perfect series of checkboxes.The Company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We highly value diversity at our company and enco

title: Principal Product Manager
department: Information Technology, Engineering, and Product Management
description_excerpt: WHO WE ARE:For enterprises struggling to secure cloud workloads, Aviatrix® offers a single solution for pervasive cloud security. Where current cybersecurity approaches focus on securing entry points to a trusted space, Aviatrix Cloud Native Security Fabric (CNSF) delivers runtime security and enforcement within the cloud application infrastructure itself – closing gaps between existing solutions and helping organizations regain visibility and control. Aviatrix ensures security, cloud, and networking teams are empowering developer velocity, AI, serverless, and what’s next. For more information, visit aviatrix.aiABOUT THE ROLE: Principal Product ManagerAs a Principal Product Manager at Aviatrix, you will serve as a recognized subject matter expert and thought leader, owning the strategy and long-term vision for a set of interrelated features and solution areas within our multi-cloud networking platform. You will drive significant initiatives that shape product value and market differentiation, influencing the product roadmap at the leadership level.Working closely with engineering, marketing, sales, and customers, you will identify emerging opportunities, make high-stakes prioritization decisions, and lead cross-functional collaboration that defines Aviatrix's competitive position across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and beyond.KEY RESPONSIBILITIESOwn and drive the long-term vision and comprehensive strategy for a set of interrelated features and solution areas, aligning them to the product's strategic goals, market position, and overall company vision.Influence the product roadmap at the leadership level by identifying future opportunities based on emerging industry trends, evolving user needs, and the competitive landscape.Engage regularly with enterprise customers, many of whom are based in the U.S., participating in product discussions, roadmap reviews, and strategic sessions; flexibility to schedule and attend meetings across time zones is expected.Establish prioritization frameworks for interdependent features that are adopted across product teams, and communicate rationale clearly to executive stakeholders.Make critical, high-stakes decisions on feature scope and strategic direction, balancing cross-functional input, market insights, and risk assessment to drive outcomes that impact the broader Product Management function.Lead execution across multiple interrelated features, overseeing project roadmaps, assigning responsibilities across teams, and establishing standards for quality delivery.Anticipate execution risks and adapt plans proactively based on resource or market changes, ensuring smooth cross-functional collaboration.Maintain deep customer focus by synthesizing feedback and real-world enterprise challenges into strategic product decisions, ensuring Aviatrix solutions address critical pain points at scale.Conduct competitive analysis and market research to keep Aviatrix at the forefront of enterprise cloud networking and security.Drive

title: Principal Product Manager
description_excerpt: Principal Product Manager - MediaSabre’s media technology platform powers the marketing campaigns that travel suppliers purchase across Sabre’s proprietary channels. With our industry‑leading Air, Lodging, Ground, and Sea businesses, along with a strong partner ecosystem, we see a significant opportunity to scale the media business over the next two years and expand our channel reach.The Media Product team works closely with customers, commercial teams, technology partners, and internal product and engineering teams. We emphasize collaboration, data‑driven decision making, and rapid learning to solve real business problems. The successful candidate will balance strategic thinking with hands‑on execution, communicate complex concepts clearly, operate with high autonomy, and influence outcomes across teams and senior stakeholders.ResponsibilitiesOwn and evolve the product vision and roadmap to support Sabre’s strategic, commercial, and growth goals for the media technology platformOwn the end‑to‑end media product lifecycle, including inventory strategy, monetization, measurement, and optimization of customer outcomesPartner with Sabre’s Media Commercial team to align product capabilities, go‑to‑market readiness, and customer demandTranslate product vision into clear requirements and user stories to enable strong engineering collaboration and value deliveryCollaborate with Product teams across Agent, Airline, and Corporate channels to enable media display, attribution, and conversion trackingSupport Sales and Account leaders in customer engagements by developing product narratives, collateral, and demos, while incorporating customer feedback into the roadmapDefine, track, and communicate customer and commercial value through dashboards, insights, and customer‑facing reportingRequirementsProven experience in Product Management, product development, and/or product deliveryExperience with digital media, advertising, sponsored content, or monetization platforms is strongly preferredStrong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts for diverse audiencesDemonstrated success in stakeholder management, including influencing senior leadership and cross‑functional teamsExperience delivering complex products in ambiguous environments with multiple dependenciesStrong track record of data‑driven decision making, including defining success metrics and evaluating trade‑offsAbility to build and sustain trusted relationships with internal teams, customers, and external partnersPassion for media, advertising technology, and the platforms that power them

title: Principal Product Manager,Support

title: Senior Director of Product Management
description_excerpt: Role OverviewWe are seeking a Senior Director of Marketing OS & Lead Generation Suite to own and scale one of the most critical product pillars. This leader will define the vision, strategy, and execution across our end-to-end lead generation and marketing operating system, ensuring our customers can attract, capture, nurture, and convert leads seamlessly.This role sits at the intersection of product, growth, and platform strategy, and will oversee a broad and highly integrated portfolio of tools that power the full customer acquisition lifecycle.Product ScopeYou will lead strategy and execution across a comprehensive suite of products, including:Lead Capture & Conversion: Funnels & Websites, Funnel AI, Forms, Surveys, Quizzes, QR Codes, Chat Widget, Countdown Timers, Funnel WidgetsTraffic & Acquisition: Digital Ads, SEO, AEO, GEO, Online Listings, Google Business Profile (GBP), Domains, URL RedirectsEngagement & Nurture: Email Marketing, Email AI, Social Planner, Blogs, Reputation Management, NotificationsGrowth & Monetization: Affiliate Manager, Loyalty & Referral ProgramsBrand & Content Systems: Template Library, Brand Boards, Media Storage, WebinarData, Analytics & Tracking: Dashboards, Reporting, Analytics, External TrackingEmerging Experiences: Vibe and next-gen AI-driven marketing workflowsKey Responsibilities1. Vision & StrategyDefine and own the long-term vision for organization’s Marketing OS and Lead Generation ecosystemIdentify opportunities to unify fragmented tools into a cohesive, AI-powered growth platformDrive product strategy that increases lead volume, conversion rates, and customer ROI2. Product LeadershipLead a group of product managers and cross-functional teams across multiple product linesEnsure tight integration across all surfaces to deliver a seamless user experiencePrioritize investments across a large surface area with clear ROI and customer impact3. Growth & MonetizationPartner with Growth, Sales, and Customer Success to drive:Adoption of core lead gen toolsExpansion revenue across the suiteMeasurable improvements in customer acquisition performanceDevelop packaging and positioning strategies for bundled and standalone offerings4. AI & Automation InnovationEmbed AI across the ecosystem (Email AI, Funnels AI, Vibe, etc.) to create autonomous marketing workflowsLead the transition from tools → intelligent systems that execute for the customer5. Platform & Ecosystem ThinkingEnsure all products operate as part of a unified Marketing OS, not standalone featuresDrive extensibility through APIs, integrations, and ecosystem partnershipsCollaborate with other platform leaders (CRM, Payments, Conversations, etc.)6. title: Head of Product - AIVM (Agentic AI & Blockchain Platform) - Asia
description_excerpt: Head of Product - AIVM (Agentic AI & Blockchain Platform)Location: Remote (Global)Position: Head of ProductCompany: AIVMPosition Type: Full-TimeAbout AIVMAIVM is building the guardrails for the post-human agentic economy, integrating cutting-edge AI and Blockchain technology. Our toolkit will enable standardized agentic payments, identity, and accountability, providing a key piece of infrastructure for the coming 1 billion AI agents. AIVM is part of the ChainGPT ecosystem, a pioneer in the decentralized AI field.About The RoleWe are looking for a highly technical and execution-driven Head of Product to lead one of the most ambitious initiatives in Web3: the AIVM (Artificial Intelligence Virtual Machine).Reporting to the CEO, you will translate AIVM's company vision into product architecture, roadmap, developer experience, and launch execution, including:The Policy Oracle: Cryptographic guardrails that intercept, evaluate, and enforce agent action before executionAgent Identity and Reputation: including emerging standards such as ERC-8004Programmable Payments and Wallet Abstraction: including x402-inspired flowsImmutable Accountability: via execution receipts that make agent actions traceable and auditableWe are currently in a high-velocity, pre-mainnet phase.This is not a conventional SaaS feature-shipping role. You will define primitives, product architecture, developer experience, and ecosystem adoption from pre-mainnet to launch.ResponsibilitiesThe immediate priority is to inherit the current roadmap, pressure-test the core primitives, align engineering and go-to-market, and drive the platform toward testnet/mainnet readiness.Product Vision & Platform StrategyInherit and evolve the existing product vision, roadmap, and platform strategy for the AIVM ecosystemPosition AIVM as a category-defining decentralized AI infrastructure layerTranslate complex technical capabilities into clear product strategy and market positioningPlatform & Ecosystem DesignLead the design of AIVM as a multi-sided platform serving:Agent developers and AI application teamsProtocols and apps integrating autonomous agentsEnterprises needing policy, compliance, and controlled executionNode operators / validators / infrastructure providersData, compute, and model providersDefine core primitives: APIs, SDKs, execution layers, and developer experienceExecution & Product LeadershipBuild and lead a high-performance product functionDrive end-to-end product execution from concept to Mainnet and beyondEstablish product processes that balance speed, quality, and scalabilityPartner deeply with engineering to align on architecture, trade-offs, and deliveryRun product and technical execution rituals to maintain roadmap clarity, accountability, and delivery momentumCross-Functional AlignmentAct as the central bridge between product, engineering, marketing, and business developmentCollaborate on go-to-market strategy, product launches, and narrativeSupport partnerships with strong product

title: Senior Director: Product Management Practice
description_excerpt: DirectorJob Title: Head of Product Management PracticeLocation: IndiaRole OverviewWe are seeking a visionary and highly strategic Head of Product Management Practice to lead, scale, and mature our Product Management consulting offering. In this pivotal leadership role, you will be the driving force behind our practice’s growth, acting as a bridge between business development, internal business units, and our talented consultants. You will define the strategic roadmap for the practice, drive revenue through client partnerships, optimize resource deployment, and cultivate a world-class environment for Product Owner and Product Manager career development.

title: Director of Product Design
description_excerpt: About EthosEthos is a leading life insurance technology company on a mission to protect families by democratizing access to life insurance and empowering agents at scale. With its robust three-sided technology platform, Ethos is transforming the life insurance experience for consumers, agents, and carriers alike. Ethos offers instant, accessible products and a seamless online process that requires no medical exams and just a few health questions; it eliminates traditional barriers, making it easier than ever for everyone to protect their families. Ethos is redefining how life insurance is bought, sold, and underwritten.About The RoleWe’re looking for a Director of Product Design to lead and elevate design for Ethos’s core business — the end-to-end journey that takes customers from landing page to policy activation and beyond. You’ll be responsible for defining how Ethos helps people navigate complex decisions with confidence, clarity, and care.You’ll collaborate with Product, Engineering, Research, and Brand leaders to ensure every user touchpoint is intuitive, efficient, and emotionally resonant with user needs and the brand.This role combines strategic vision, team management, with hands-on design and product building excellence. You’ll champion intentional, intuitive, and engaging design, compelling storytelling, strategic experimentation, and measurable business impact — and will help make Ethos the most trusted and user-friendly life insurance experience in the world.Duties And ResponsibilitiesLead design for the core conversion funnel — covering landing experiences, application flows, underwriting, checkout, onboarding, and policy management.Lead, mentor, and grow a small but mighty team of product designers.Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Research, Marketing, and Brand to deliver cohesive experiences that delight users and align with key business outcomes such as conversion and retention.Raise the bar for the design practice across: design systems and craft, accessibility,decision-making discipline, experimentation, storytelling, leverage of research and data.Partner with adjacent design functions, such as Performance Creative and Brand, to create a consistent, connected user journey across growth and product surfaces.Contribute to create an environment of excellence and growth where team members can do their best work and grow professionally and personally. Qualifications10+ years of product design (UX) experience, including several years leading multi-person design teams.Proven ability to design for complex, decision-heavy user journeys in b2c journeys. industries like fintech, healthtech, or insurtech.Strong understanding of conversion optimization, user motivation, and behavior design principles.Demonstrated experience turning ambiguity into clarity — structuring complex information and simplifying it into elegant, actionable flows.Skilled at storytelling and stakeholder communication — you make design rationa

title: Director, Product Management- Data Threat Analytics
description_excerpt: Director, Product Management, Data Threat AnalyticsLocation: Bangalore, IndiaAbout The TeamThe Product Management team at Rubrik drives Rubrik’s vision forward, ensuring we continue to secure the world’s data by delivering on our market-leading platform to address our customers’ most challenging data management & security needs. Our Product Managers are responsible for guiding multidisciplinary teams towards the goal of shipping high-quality products on time. We wear multiple hats throughout the different stages in the product lifecycle and set the vision for our growing family of products. As advocates for both our users and our business, Product Managers balance varied perspectives and empower their teams to do great work.About The RoleThis role requires a leader who can set a compelling strategy, align diverse teams, and turn complex recovery problems into clear, actionable outcomes.For this, we are seeking a Director of Product Management - Data Threat Analytics with a strong hybrid and cloud security background to lead a product area (and PMs) focused on detecting and understanding threats against sensitive data in hybrid and cloud environments - with recovery as the end goal. This role owns anomaly detection, threat hunting, monitoring, and sensitive data discovery on backed-up data, and will help reimagine cyber recovery for hybrid and modern cloud architectures.What You’ll OwnProduct Strategy: Define and own the long-term product vision for data threat analytics anchored in hybrid, and cloud-native environments, and recovery-first outcomes.Reimagine how threat signals, anomaly detection, and sensitive data discovery directly inform - blast radius assessment, clean restore point selection and how this feeds into confident cyber recovery workflows Develop a point of view on the evolving hybrid and cloud threat landscape, including ransomware, and misconfigurations - and translate into differentiated product strategyExecution ExcellenceDrive end-to-end execution for recovery across matrix organization and own the process from concept validation to GA and customer adoption while holding the upstream teams accountableBreak-down ambiguous, multi-year product bets into clear milestones, measurable outcomes, and incremental valueEstablish strong product operating rhythms - with tight roadmap discipline and backlog hygienePeople & Org LeadershipBuild, lead and scale a high-performing team of Product ManagersCoach PMs to think beyond feature delivery toward - problem framing, systems thinking and recovery outcomes.Develop PM talent with strong focus on structured thinking and crisp communication, executive-ready storytelling and customer-centric results.Foster a culture of high accountability with stakeholders and build teams that move fast, learn, and course-correct.Create clarity in a complex org by defining ownership boundaries, decision-making frameworks, and clear interfaces with engineering, snappable PMs, data science and security research.

title: Director, Product Design
description_excerpt: About UsHighLevel is an AI-powered business operating system that gives agencies, entrepreneurs and SMBs the infrastructure to build, automate and scale. Today, HighLevel supports SMBs across 150+ countries, fueling community-driven growth rooted in real customer outcomes.To date, businesses operating on HighLevel have generated over $7 billion in ecosystem value, demonstrating the impact of shared infrastructure at scale. By centralizing conversations, automation and intelligence into one system, we help businesses move faster, reduce complexity and execute efficiently.Behind the platform, HighLevel powers more than 4 billion API hits and 2.5 billion message events daily. With 250 terabytes of distributed data, 250+ microservices and over 1 million domain names supported, our architecture is built for performance, resilience and long-term scalability.Our peopleWith over 2,000 team members across 10+ countries, HighLevel operates as a global, remote-first organization built for speed and ownership. We value initiative, clarity and execution, creating space for ambitious people to build systems that support millions of businesses worldwide. Here, innovation thrives, ideas are celebrated and people come first, no matter where they call home.Our impactEvery month, HighLevel enables more than 1.5 billion messages, 200 million leads and 20 million conversations for the more than 1 million businesses we support. Behind those numbers are real people building independence, expanding opportunity and creating measurable impact. We’re proud to be a part of that.Learn more about us on our YouTube Channel or Blog PostsThe RoleWe’re looking for a Director of Design, Growth Products to lead and scale design across our most critical growth experiences. This role owns the end-to-end design strategy for key growth surfaces including new user onboarding, product activation, expansion experiences, and cancellation/retention flows .You will partner closely with Product, Engineering, Data, and Marketing to drive measurable business impact while delivering intuitive, high-quality user experiences. This is a hands-on leadership role for someone who thrives at the intersection of strategy, craft, experimentation, and people leadership .What You’ll Own Design strategy and execution for growth-focused product areas , including: New user onboarding and first-run experiences Product activation and adoption flows Monetization, upgrade, and retention experiences Cancellation, downgrade, and save flows The design vision and roadmap for growth products, ensuring alignment with company goals and user needs A team of product designers, including hiring, coaching, performance management, and career development Design quality, consistency, and usability across growth touchpoints A strong experimentation culture—leveraging A/B testing, data, and qualitative insights to continuously optimize experiencesResponsibilities Lead and mentor a team of designers focused on growth and lifecycl

title: Director of Product Security
description_excerpt: Role OverviewThe Director – Product & Cloud Security owns the strategy, architecture, and execution of security across the product lifecycle and cloud platforms, ensuring that Odessa’s SaaS products are secure by design, compliant by default, and resilient at scale.This role leads Product Security, Cloud Security, and AI Security integration efforts, working closely with Engineering, Architecture, Product Management, DevOps/SRE, Compliance, and Legal teams. title: Principal Agilist - Product Area
description_excerpt: Candescent is a forward-thinking technology company transforming how financial institutions deliver Intelligent Banking experiences. We unite digital banking, account opening, and branch solutions that power and connect digital banking, account opening, and branch solutions—creating seamless engagement across digital, remote, and in-person channels.Our Experience-Led, Intelligence-Driven approach combines human-centered design with data, automation, and cloud-based innovation. Built on an API-first architecture, our extensible ecosystem enables institutions to adapt quickly, integrate easily, and unlock new opportunities for growth—turning every customer interaction into a moment of clarity, confidence, and connection.Job Title: Principal Agilist - Product AreaLevel: Portfolio (multi-product area)Location: BangaloreReports to: VP, Head of AgileOverviewThe Principal Agilist manages and grows the Agilist discipline across an entire portfolio area. This role directly leads Product Agilists and Team Agilists, ensuring consistent practices, strong coaching, and a culture of continuous improvement across all products. Acting as the functional counterpart to Product Management, Design, and Engineering leadership, the Lead Agilist drives organizational flow, resolves systemic impediments, and ensures agility is demonstrated at scale. In partnership with the Agile CoE Leader, this role contributes to designing and delivering business transformation.Key ResponsibilitiesManage, mentor, and develop Product Agilists and Team Agilists across all products in the portfolio area. Establish consistent Agile practices, standards, and playbooks, guiding teams and products in adoption, adaptation, and improvement. Partner with Product, Design, and Engineering leadership to improve flow, remove systemic impediments, and proactively manage risks.
